title: Legislative Relations & Doctrines
lead: The judicially crafted toolkit for reconciling Union and State legislative power — plenary powers, ==harmonious construction==, ==pith and substance== and ==colourable legislation==.

items: Autonomy to Centre and States over their legislative fields — [[Javed v. State of Haryana (2003)]].
Entries should ==not be read narrowly== — [[State of WB v. UoI (1963)]].
Legislatures can make ==retrospective and prospective== laws — [[Rai Ramkrishna v. Bihar (1963)]].
But ==no power to undo an individual court decision== affecting parties' rights — [[I.N. Saksena v. MP (1976)]].

items.title: Liberal & Harmonious Construction
bullets: No watertight division — courts ==reconcile conflicting entries==

[[ITC Ltd v. APMC (2002)]]: entries are ==‘fields of legislation’, not powers== — competency traces to the Constitution; read entries together; preserve federalism ==while upholding central supremacy==
[[GJ University v. KR Mudholkar (1963)]]: Gujarat couldn't prescribe medium of instruction — interfered with Union's power over ==standards in higher education==

items.title: Ancillary / Incidental Powers
bullets: Power over a topic includes ==reasonably included ancillary matters== — [[United Provinces v. Atiqa Begum (1941)]] (land → mortgages of land)
Punitive measures enforcing social legislation are ancillary — [[Joshi v. Mills (1977)]]
Limits: power to ==levy tax does NOT include power to confiscate== goods

items.title: Pith & Substance
bullets: When a law on one list ==touches a subject in another list==
[[Prafulla Kumar v. Bank of Commerce (1947)]]: consider ==object, scope, effect==; extent of invasion goes to characterisation, not validity; ==List I > II & III; List III > II==
[[M Ismail Faruqui v. UoI (1995)]]: pith and substance was ==acquisition of property==, not public order — Act valid

items.title: Colourable Legislation
bullets: ==‘You cannot do indirectly what you cannot do directly’== — disguised, covert transgression
[[KC Gajapati Deo v. Orissa (1953)]]: about ==competency, not intent== (bona fides irrelevant)
[[State of Bihar v. Kameshwar Singh (1952)]]: Bihar Land Reforms Act ==struck down==
